title: juncus alpinoarticulatus subsp. rariflorus
intro: Juncus alpinoarticulatus subsp. rariflorus, also known as alpine joint rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rush family Juncaceae. It is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. Juncus alpinoarticulatus subsp. rariflorus is often prepared as a tea or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties, which may also help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being.
